sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: AMN Healthcare Services, Inc. (NYSE:AMN) Q4 2025 earnings call transcript
feed.summary: This document is an earnings call transcript for AMN Healthcare Services, Inc. (NYSE:AMN) for the fourth quarter of 2025. It details the discussions held during the company's investor call regarding its financial performance and outlook.

feed.title: Alector (ALEC) CEO awarded 116,246 RSUs, boosts direct share stake
feed.summary: Alector, Inc. CEO Arnon Rosenthal was granted 116,246 restricted stock units (RSUs) on March 13, 2026, as equity compensation. These RSUs, which have no cash cost, will vest in two equal installments on September 1, 2026, and March 1, 2027. This grant increases his direct ownership to 2,454,422 common shares, in addition to indirect holdings through family trusts.

feed.title: Could AIRO Group Holdings' Drone Focus and Vertical Integration Redefine Its Defense Narrative (AIRO)?
feed.summary: AIRO Group Holdings is emphasizing its defense-oriented drones, proprietary tech, and vertical integration to build recurring revenue in defense and air mobility. The company's recent presentation highlighted progress in U.S.-based Blue UAS aligned production, crucial for converting its significant bookings into recognized revenue. Investors are monitoring potential certification delays and the company's ambitious revenue and earnings forecasts which project a substantial upside from its current price.

feed.title: QUALCOMM Incorporated Stock (ISIN: US7475251036) Under Pressure from Insider Sales and Bearish Forecasts
feed.summary: QUALCOMM Incorporated (ISIN: US7475251036) stock is currently trading around $130, facing pressure from recent insider sales by executives and bearish short-term forecasts. The stock has seen a 5% decline in the past week, with technical indicators signaling a sell and short-term models predicting further dips. Despite these concerns, some analysts maintain a "buy" rating, anticipating long-term potential in 5G and AI sectors, though geopolitical and market headwinds remain.

feed.title: China Obesity Approval Puts Pfizer’s GLP 1 Bet In Sharper Focus
feed.summary: Pfizer has received approval from China for its GLP-1 obesity therapy, Xianweiying (ecnoglutide), marking a significant step in expanding its non-COVID revenue streams. This therapy, obtained from Sciwind Biosciences, positions Pfizer in the rapidly growing weight management drug market in China, diversifying its portfolio. Investors will be closely watching the commercial rollout, pricing, and market share performance, especially given the competitive landscape with established players like Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly.

feed.title: AT&T Inc Stock (ISIN: US0019571090) Unveils $250 Billion Infrastructure Push Amid Tariff Wars
feed.summary: AT&T Inc. has announced a bold $250 billion investment plan through 2030 to overhaul its infrastructure, focusing on fiber, mobile, and satellite networks, alongside new budget-friendly tariffs. This strategic move aims to intensify competition with Verizon and T-Mobile in the US wireless market and is closely watched by European investors for its potential to redefine value in the telecom sector. The company reaffirmed its strong FY 2026 EPS guidance, but the large-scale investment raises questions about execution risks and potential shareholder dilution.

feed.title: Assessing Hubbell (HUBB) Valuation After A 9% Pullback And Strong 1 Year Return
feed.summary: Hubbell (HUBB) shares have experienced a 9% pullback over the last month, yet they still show a 39% total return over the past year. While one popular narrative suggests the stock is 12.3% undervalued with a fair value of $532.85, primarily due to grid-focused revenue growth, Simply Wall St's DCF model indicates a fair value closer to $343.63, suggesting the current share price of $467.38 might be expensive. Investors are encouraged to examine underlying data and consider both the optimistic narrative and the more cautious DCF signals, along with identified risks like tariff and cost inflation.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines Stock Faces Headwinds Amid Capacity Cuts and Cost Pressures
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (ISIN: US8447411088) is experiencing operational and market challenges leading to capacity cuts and cost pressures. The stock is trading near recent lows, reflecting investor concerns about the airline's low-cost model resilience amidst rising fuel costs and softening leisure demand. Despite these headwinds, the company maintains a solid balance sheet and has reinstated dividends, though its outlook remains cautious among analysts.

feed.title: Union Pacific Corp Stock (ISIN: US9078181081) Gains Traction as Analysts Upgrade and Institutions Pile In
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corp (UNP) stock is gaining significant investor interest due to increased institutional buying and optimistic analyst upgrades. With shares trading within a 52-week range of $204.66 to $268.14, Baird recently upgraded the stock to Outperform with a $311 target, citing strong cost synergies. Institutional investors like Prudential PLC and Pinnbrook Capital Management have boosted their stakes, signifying confidence in UNP's operational leverage and its Precision Scheduled Railroading model amidst an anticipated US rail recovery.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Cruises: Record Demand and Fleet Expansion Drive 2026 Momentum
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group reported strong Q4 revenue of $4.26 billion, driven by robust booking trends and new ship deliveries, positioning the company for continued growth in a recovering travel market. The company is aggressively expanding its fleet with LNG-powered ships like Icon of the Seas, and its financial health is strong with a net margin of 23.80% and significant free cash flow. Despite potential risks like fuel price fluctuations and geopolitical tensions, strategic differentiation and diversified itineraries are expected to sustain its leadership in experiential travel.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines dropping service to 2 major US airports
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines announced it will discontinue service to Washington, D.C.'s Dulles International Airport and Chicago's O’Hare International Airport starting June 4, 2026. All flights booked after this date to and from these airports will be canceled, with customers eligible for refunds. The airline will continue to serve the D.C. and Chicago areas via Baltimore-Washington International, Ronald Reagan Washington National, and Chicago Midway International airports.

feed.title: S&P Global Inc Stock (ISIN: US78409V1044) Gains on AI Enhancements and Strong Analyst Outlook
feed.summary: S&P Global Inc. stock is performing strongly, bolstered by recent AI platform upgrades to its Capital IQ Pro service and a positive outlook from analysts. The company's strategic focus on integrating AI-driven tools, such as ProntoNLP and Drift AI, into its financial data and analytics offerings positions it for continued growth in institutional workflows. Analysts maintain a consensus "buy" rating with significant upside potential, citing expected earnings growth and a resilient business model supported by diversified revenue streams and a strong balance sheet.

feed.title: UnitedHealth Group Inc Stock (ISIN: US91324P1021) Recovers Amid Volatility and Solid Dividend Outlook
feed.summary: UnitedHealth Group Inc (UNH) stock experienced a 1.82% gain to $282.09 on March 13, 2026, bouncing back despite sector volatility and regulatory challenges. The recovery is supported by growing institutional confidence, fresh analyst backing, and an upcoming $2.21 dividend. Analysts rate UNH a 'Moderate Buy' with a target of $372.13, highlighting its dual-engine model (UnitedHealthcare and Optum) for sustained growth.

feed.title: New to The Street Episode #737 Airs on Bloomberg Television Across the United States, Latin America, and MENA at 6:30 PM EST
feed.summary: New to The Street will broadcast Episode #737 on Bloomberg Television on Saturday, March 14, 2026, at 6:30 PM EST, reaching audiences across the United States, Latin America, and MENA. The episode features interviews with executives from FLOKI, KLED.AI, Sagtech Global, Medicus Pharma, and YY Group, exploring innovations in technology, digital ecosystems, and healthcare. Additionally, the broadcast will include national television commercial campaigns from publicly traded companies like NRx Pharmaceuticals and Stardust Power Inc., expanding their brand visibility.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. (NASDAQ:JBHT) Short Interest Down 23.5% in February
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) saw a 23.5% decrease in short interest during February, with 2.5% of its shares sold short and a short-interest ratio of 3.1 days. Insider selling totaled approximately $5.66 million last quarter, although a senior executive made small open-market purchases. The company recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.45, and analysts currently have a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $203.

feed.title: Amkor Technology Declares Dividend Reinforcing Investor Confidence
feed.summary: Amkor Technology (AMKR) has declared a dividend of $0.08352 per share, signaling strong financial health and consistent cash flow. The company exhibits robust financial fundamentals with a manageable debt-to-equity ratio, stable gross and EBITDA margins, and a competitive P/E ratio, despite a declining three-year revenue trajectory. While the stock has shown sideways consolidation, maintaining above $43.15 could indicate a bullish breakout, and experts hold a cautiously positive sentiment on its medium-term prospects.

feed.title: Short Interest in Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE:VZ) Declines By 23.5%
feed.summary: Short interest in Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE:VZ) decreased by 23.5% in February, reflecting a reduction in bearish sentiment. The company reported strong Q4 earnings, raised its dividend, and received analyst price target upgrades, contributing to positive investor outlook. Despite insider selling, institutional ownership remains strong, with major firms increasing their stakes.

feed.title: ADC Therapeutics signals ZYNLONTA peak revenue potential of $600M–$1B in U.S. with expanded clinical milestones
feed.summary: ADC Therapeutics announced that ZYNLONTA, its approved drug for relapsed or refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), has a projected peak revenue potential in the U.S. of $600 million to $1 billion. The company also presented new clinical data and outlined plans for further development, including combination therapies and studies in new indications. These initiatives aim to expand ZYNLONTA's market reach and patient population.

feed.title: Sharpen Your Knives: It’s the Final Round of Northern California’s Battle of the Blades
feed.summary: Sysco Corporation is hosting the "Battle of the Blades Grand Finale," a live cooking contest to crown Sysco NorCal's Culinary Artist of the Year. Three finalists will compete in a timed challenge, judged on visual appeal, creativity, execution, ingredient use, and taste. The event will take place on March 16, 2026, at the Culinary Institute of America at Copia in Napa, California.

feed.title: Aldeyra Awaits FDA Verdict On Dry Eye Drug Reproxalap
feed.summary: Aldeyra Therapeutics (ALDX) is awaiting a crucial FDA decision on March 16, 2026, for its dry eye drug candidate, Reproxalap. The company has resubmitted its NDA after previous rejections, and a new Phase 3 trial showed promising results in reducing ocular discomfort. If approved, Reproxalap would enter a competitive market but could differentiate itself with its rapid onset of action.

feed.title: YCS ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:YCS
feed.summary: The article provides an in-depth analysis of the ProShares UltraShort Yen New (YCS) ETF, focusing on its dividends, returns, and key characteristics. YCS offers 2x leveraged inverse exposure to the Japanese yen/US dollar cross, designed for short-term trading. It highlights the fund's assets under management, expense ratio, and replication method, emphasizing that YCS does not pay dividends.

feed.title: Innovator Equity Defined Protection ETF - 1 Yr April (ZAPR) Institutional Ownership 2026
feed.summary: This article details the institutional ownership of Innovator Equity Defined Protection ETF - 1 Yr April (ZAPR) for 2026. It provides data on institutional buying and selling over the last 12 and 24 months, including the number of buyers/sellers and total inflow/outflow of funds. The report also lists major shareholders and their ownership history, along with answers to frequently asked questions about ZAPR's institutional investors.

feed.title: GSK’s RSV vaccine wins broader FDA clearance
feed.summary: The FDA has expanded the approval for GSK's RSV vaccine, Arexvy, to include adults aged 18 to 49 with increased risk of severe RSV disease. This decision significantly broadens the vaccine's potential market and intensifies competition with Pfizer's Abrysvo and Moderna's mResvia. The approval comes amidst a challenging regulatory landscape for vaccine makers in the U.S., marked by declining sales and changing government policies on immunizations.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines drops service to Chicago, Washington D.C. airports
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines announced it will discontinue service to Washington D.C.'s Dulles International Airport and Chicago's O'Hare International Airport. This change will take effect starting June 4. The article provides only this detail regarding Southwest Airlines' decision.

feed.title: How Low Can JCI Really Go In A Market Crash?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Johnson Controls International (JCI) stock performance during 15 major market dislocations, noting an average -23% contraction compared to the S&P 500's -16%. It identifies "Credit & Liquidity Crises" as JCI's greatest vulnerability, leading to an average -32% decline. The piece details JCI's reactions to various historical market shocks, including the 2008 Global Financial Crisis, the 2020 COVID-19 crash, and the 2022 Fed Tightening, to help investors understand the stock's downside risk.

feed.title: Intel Corporation Stock (ISIN: US4581401001) Faces Shareholder Lawsuit Amid Strong Recovery and Chip Shortage Tailwinds
feed.summary: Intel Corporation is facing a shareholder lawsuit alleging the company granted a 10% equity stake to the U.S. government to mitigate political pressure. Despite this legal challenge, Intel's stock has shown strong recovery, fueled by new product launches, ongoing chip demand, and its foundry ambitions. The company's Q4 2025 results beat revenue estimates, but Q1 2026 guidance was cautious, reflecting its transition from legacy chips to AI and data center growth, supported by government subsidies and European expansion plans.

feed.title: Centene Corp. Stock (ISIN: US15133V1035) Faces Pressure Amid Institutional Buying and Analyst Caution
feed.summary: Centene Corp. stock (ISIN: US15133V1035) experienced a slight dip on March 13, 2026, extending a five-day losing streak despite increased positions by institutional investors like Quinn Opportunity Partners and Private Management Group. While TD Cowen raised its price target to $38, analyst sentiment remains cautious, with the broader market and regulatory shifts weighing on the managed care giant. The article discusses Centene's business model, institutional confidence, and risks like policy changes, appealing to patient European investors seeking defensive healthcare exposure.

feed.title: PepsiCo, Inc. Stock (ISIN: US7134481081) Holds Steady Amid Mixed Analyst Views and Solid Dividend Ap
feed.summary: PepsiCo stock closed at $159.88 on March 13, 2026, showing stability driven by recent earnings beats and a robust dividend policy, despite mixed analyst views. The company's low beta of 0.39 offers defensive appeal, particularly for European investors, and its diversified business model and commitment to innovation support long-term growth. While analysts maintain a "Hold" consensus, the stock offers a 3.6% dividend yield and modest appreciation potential.

feed.title: Ventas Inc Stock (ISIN: US9182041080) Faces Mixed Signals as Healthcare Real Estate Sector Navigates Rate Uncertainty
feed.summary: Ventas Inc (ISIN: US9182041080), a prominent healthcare REIT, is navigating a complex environment characterized by fluctuating interest rates and refinancing pressures, impacting its valuation. While the company's diversified portfolio and stable dividend appeal to income investors, particularly from Europe and DACH regions, the near-term outlook is marked by volatility due to elevated capital costs and operator health concerns. The stock is considered suitable for conservative, income-seeking investors but lacks significant growth potential, aligning with its fair pricing as a stable, low-growth asset.

feed.title: Kroger Co Stock (ISIN: US4963911080) Faces Pressure Amid Supermarket Sector Headwinds
feed.summary: Kroger Co stock is currently facing pressure due to cooling US grocery inflation and increased competition, leading investors to re-evaluate the retail giant's growth prospects. The company, the largest US supermarket operator, is experiencing downward momentum as price-sensitive shoppers favor discounters. Despite generating robust free cash flow and a progressive dividend, Kroger's pure-play grocery model makes it vulnerable to food price dynamics in a low-inflation environment, requiring long-term patience for market share gains.

feed.title: Dow Inc. Stock (ISIN: US2605571031) Faces Pressure Amid Analyst Upgrades and Market Volatility
feed.summary: Dow Inc. (ISIN: US2605571031) experienced a sharp decline on March 13, 2026, despite multiple analyst upgrades and a generally positive year-to-date performance. The stock's underperformance, relative to the S&P 500, reflects ongoing market volatility, sector headwinds, and geopolitical risks, even as analysts like Wells Fargo and Citigroup raised price targets based on expected margin expansion and volume stabilization. European investors are noted to consider Dow as a diversification play against local chemicals producers, appreciating its global footprint and dividend yield.

feed.title: Coca-Cola UNITED putting campus of Birmingham plant on market ahead of move to new headquarters
feed.summary: Coca-Cola Bottling Company UNITED is selling its 39-acre campus in Birmingham, located on East Lake Boulevard, ahead of its relocation to a new corporate headquarters. The current campus, built in 1979 and renovated in 2004, features 350,000 square feet of office and industrial space. The company is moving to a new 100-acre facility along I-59/20 in Birmingham's Kingston community, a $330 million project expected to be completed in late 2026, creating 50 new jobs and retaining over 750 existing ones.

feed.title: Booking Holdings Genius Program: Loyalty Revolution in Online Travel
feed.summary: Booking Holdings' Genius program is a crucial loyalty initiative for Booking.com, significantly driving customer retention and revenue growth in the competitive online travel market. The program, which offers tiered perks, has seen increased adoption and contributes over 30% of total bookings on the platform, attracting higher-spending travelers. Investors see the Genius program as a key indicator of sustainable growth and market share expansion for Booking Holdings, with plans for further expansion into flights and car rentals.

feed.title: Andersons (NASDAQ:ANDE) Hits New 52-Week High
feed.summary: The Andersons, Inc. (NASDAQ:ANDE) recently reached a new 52-week high of $71.15, closing at $70.56, demonstrating strong investor confidence. This surge follows robust quarterly earnings, with EPS of $2.04 beating estimates despite an 18.8% revenue decline year-over-year. Analysts have largely maintained positive outlooks, with BMO Capital Markets increasing its price target to $75.

feed.title: AGL - Agilon Health Inc Latest Stock News & Market Updates
feed.summary: This page provides the latest news and analysis for Agilon Health (AGL), focusing on updates relevant to investors and traders. It aggregates company-specific news including financial results, leadership changes, investor conference participation, and research related to its value-based healthcare model for senior patients. Key announcements include Q1 2021 financial results and details of its initial public offering.

feed.title: Village Super Market, Inc. Declares Quarterly Cash Dividends for Class A and Class B Shares
feed.summary: Village Super Market, Inc. announced its Board of Directors has declared quarterly cash dividends of $0.25 per Class A common share and $0.1625 per Class B common share. These dividends are payable on April 23, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 2, 2026. The company operates 34 supermarkets and three specialty markets in New York City, and saw a 6.89% revenue increase in Q2 2026, reaching $641 million.

feed.title: Bunge Global SA Stock (ISIN: BMG169621056) Gains Traction as Agriculture Sector Momentum Builds
feed.summary: Bunge Global SA (BMG169621056) is experiencing renewed investor interest within the agriculture sector, driven by rising global food demand and a significant decline in short interest. The company, a major agribusiness and food processor, benefits from its integrated model spanning oilseed processing, grain trading, and food ingredients, positioning it favorably amid biofuel tailwinds and stabilizing sentiment. This trend is particularly relevant for European investors seeking inflation-hedging qualities and diversified commodity exposure.

feed.title: Walmart names Cox as chief legal officer
feed.summary: Walmart Inc. has appointed Erin Nealy Cox as its new chief legal officer and corporate secretary, also making her executive vice president of global governance. Cox, previously a partner at Kirkland & Ellis LLP and a U.S. Attorney for the Northern District of Texas, brings extensive experience in legal strategy and federal investigations. John Furner, Walmart's CEO, highlighted her legal and operational expertise as crucial for the company's future.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on PepsiCo: “I Don’t Want You Selling”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer advised against selling PepsiCo (NASDAQ: PEP) despite short-term concerns, highlighting its 3.5% yield, strong leadership by CEO Ramon Laguarta, and position as a leading food and beverage play. While Cramer acknowledged potential struggles in PepsiCo's snack division due to GLP-1 weight loss drugs, he suggested the company might adapt its strategy. He emphasized that investors should not sell "good stocks" based on transient market worries.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Suggests Berkshire Hathaway and Chubb Over The Progressive Corporation
feed.summary: Jim Cramer advised investors to consider Berkshire Hathaway and Chubb over The Progressive Corporation (NYSE: PGR) for insurance sector exposure. He noted that Berkshire Hathaway offers a more diversified approach with GEICO, and Chubb is a superior company. Cramer's comments came in response to a caller's query about The Progressive Corporation's stock.

feed.title: Activist Starboard is said to build $350 million stake in CarMax
feed.summary: Activist investor Starboard Value LP has reportedly built a $350 million stake in CarMax Inc., the used-car retailer. This move suggests that Starboard may push for changes or better performance at the company. The specific intentions of the activist fund are not yet clear.

feed.title: How Investors Are Reacting To Gartner (IT) AI Summit Spotlight Amid New Earnings Disclosure Investigation
feed.summary: Gartner's recent AI Summit highlighted its thought leadership in AI readiness and data management, but investor focus is divided. The company faces a securities law investigation into its Q4 2025 earnings disclosures, which has raised questions about corporate transparency and financial reporting quality. This investigation, coupled with a revenue miss and a sharp share price drop, has shifted investor sentiment, while the long-term impact of free AI tools on Gartner's pricing power remains a concern.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Remains a Buy on Alphabet Class A (GOOGL)
feed.summary: Evercore ISI maintained a Buy rating on Alphabet Class A (GOOGL) with a price target of $400.00, according to analyst Mark Mahaney. Alphabet's latest earnings report showed significant revenue and net profit growth compared to the previous year. While Needham also issued a Buy rating, TipRanks – xAI recently downgraded GOOGL to a Hold.

feed.title: Assessing Diamondback Energy (FANG) Valuation After Strong Recent Share Price Momentum
feed.summary: Diamondback Energy (FANG) has seen strong recent share price momentum, with positive returns over the past year and three years. While a "most popular narrative" suggests slight overvaluation at $182.37 against a fair value of $179.03, the Simply Wall St Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model indicates deep undervaluation with a fair value of $500.19. Investors are advised to consider both perspectives and evaluate the full picture, including key rewards and warning signs, before making investment decisions.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says “I Do Want to Own Eaton”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed his desire to own Eaton Corporation plc (NYSE: ETN), acknowledging that current geopolitical events might make investors hesitant. Eaton, a power management company, is benefiting from strong demand in data centers and upgraded electric grids, leading to increased sales and improved margins. Despite recent underperformance, analysts believe Eaton has strong long-term demand due to ongoing electrification and grid investment, alongside AI infrastructure spending.

feed.title: Is There Now An Opportunity In Becton Dickinson (BDX) After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: Becton Dickinson (BDX) has seen recent share price weakness, with declines across multiple timeframes. Despite this, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 27.1%, and its P/E ratio also indicates it might be undervalued when compared to a proprietary "Fair Ratio." The article encourages investors to use valuation narratives to assess the stock's potential.

feed.title: Is There Now An Opportunity In Becton Dickinson (BDX) After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: Becton Dickinson's stock has seen recent weakness, with declines across various timeframes. Despite this, a Discounted Cash Flow analysis suggests BDX is undervalued by 27.1%, and its P/E ratio compared to a proprietary "Fair Ratio" also indicates it might be undervalued. feed.title: Jim Cramer Says He Would “Stick With Bank of America”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer recommends sticking with Bank of America (NYSE: BAC), highlighting its strong long-term return potential despite recent market pressures. He praises the bank's "astounding" Q4 2025 earnings, which showed a small top and bottom-line beat, 7% revenue growth, and 18% earnings per share growth, along with a confident outlook for 2026. Cramer dismissed the stock's recent decline as "guilt by association" rather than a reflection of its strong performance.

feed.title: Bimzelx Trial Win Raises New Questions For AbbVie Skyrizi Outlook
feed.summary: UCB's Bimzelx outperformed AbbVie's Skyrizi in a head-to-head clinical trial for psoriatic arthritis, posing a new competitive challenge for AbbVie's key immunology product. While early indications suggest a limited immediate sales impact, investors are closely monitoring how this will affect Skyrizi's future positioning and market share. This development adds to existing concerns for AbbVie, such as its high debt load and dividend coverage, particularly if it impacts a core immunology revenue stream.

feed.title: Henrietta Lacks family settles lawsuit against Viatris over use of her cells
feed.summary: The family of Henrietta Lacks has settled their lawsuit against generic drugmaker Viatris, resolving another legal dispute concerning the unauthorized use of her cells. This settlement follows previous agreements with Novartis and Thermo Fisher, as the family continues to seek justice for Lacks' invaluable contribution to medical research without her consent. Lacks' cells, known as HeLa cells, were taken in 1951 without her knowledge and have since become a cornerstone of scientific advancement.
